name: the-extended-phenotype-the-long-reach-of-the-gene
description: Richard Dawkins' revolutionary concept that a gene's effects extend beyond the body — spider webs, beaver dams, bird nests, and parasite manipulation are all extended phenotypes.
useCase: Understand the extended phenotype concept — how genes reach beyond the body to shape the environment, animal artifacts, and even manipulate other species.
knownRisks: Dense and theoretical — Dawkins' most academic work
Requires basic understanding of evolutionary biology
Some concepts may seem counterintuitive or controversial
